authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal rule for cGMP, monitoring, corrective action, verification, and recordkeeping in human food manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-manufacturing quality evidence and control review

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's rule page is the official workflow summary for covered facilities and points directly to current guidance, applicability boundaries, and supply-chain materials used in food-manufacturing quality work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: facility applicability and preventive-controls workflow framing

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for traceability lot codes, shipping and receiving KDEs, and additional recordkeeping obligations for covered foods.

authoritative_sources.title: Supply-Chain Program Requirements and Co-Manufacturer Supplier Approval and Verification for Human Food and Animal Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's guidance is authoritative public workflow guidance for supplier approval, verification-activity selection, receiving procedures, and co-manufacturer handling under the preventive-controls rules.

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's recall guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when affected food must move from ordinary quality review into formal recall or incident escalation handling.

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code: Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI publishes a widely recognized food-manufacturing code that is directly relevant to system elements, GMPs, nonconforming product, corrective action, and documented plant quality discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: plant quality-system and nonconformance workflow handling

authoritative_sources.title: Quality Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI's quality-code materials are a recognized public operating reference for monitoring and controlling food quality-related threats once a food-safety plan exists, which directly matches this quality-operations lane.
used_for: quality-code framing for findings and escalation

authoritative_sources.title: General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Codex is the globally recognized public baseline for food hygiene and HACCP vocabulary, including corrective action, verification, documentation, and lot-marking expectations used across food manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food hygiene and HACCP terminology review

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1's public global traceability standard is a recognized vendor-neutral workflow reference for linking lot, shipment, receipt, and transformation data across supply-chain systems of record.

workflow_stages.name: food-manufacturing quality evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review batch, lot, inspection, retained-sample, supplier, and release records to determine whether the visible evidence supports a bounded quality recommendation.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and nonconformance boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold and release boundaries, identify unresolved evidence gaps, and route final authority to QA, supplier-quality, engineering, or recall-command owners.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: traceability and inventory conflict analysis
workflow_stages.description: Analyze lot, batch, shipment, receiving, transformation, and inventory records across ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and traceability repositories to determine whether lineage and hold scope are supportable.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: supplier-quality and corrective-action escalation
workflow_stages.description: Assess supplier approval, receiving controls, COA support, repeat defects, and incident severity to decide when supplier-quality escalation or formal corrective-action routing is required.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: production orders, batch records, and lot genealogy logs
in-process inspection results, fill-weight or package-integrity checks, retained-sample references, and finished-goods review records
quality holds, release records, nonconformance reports, deviation investigations, CAPA packages, and change-control records
supplier approval records, certificates of analysis, receiving inspection evidence, and supplier corrective-action responses
inventory states, warehouse status logs, shipping and receiving KDEs, and traceability lot code records
complaint, repeat-defect, and incident summaries tied to affected lots or distributed product

allowed: assess whether visible quality or safety evidence supports hold, release recommendation, investigation, corrective-action escalation, or findings packaging
organize missing records, unresolved lot scope, and required next owners
summarize supplier, traceability, packaging, retained-sample, or verification conflicts without overstating authority
escalate: final QA release, recall command, or regulator-facing communication
supplier approval, substitution approval, or use-as-is authority
engineering or process changes that alter validated controls or product specifications
legal, customer-contract, or jurisdiction-specific interpretations that materially change the answer
refuse: override documented hold, release, traceability, or quality controls
treat incomplete batch, lot, supplier, inspection, or review records as acceptable proof of release or closure
approve rework, substitution, or nonconformance closure where formal quality or supplier approval is still required

domain_failure_modes: traceability records conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, batch, or supplier evidence
product is pressured toward release while hold, deviation, or review evidence is incomplete
repeat defects or incidents are treated as administratively closed without corrective-action evidence
supplier substitutions or COA exceptions are accepted without documented authority
quality review drifts into final release, recall command, engineering changes, or legal interpretation

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats jurisdiction as a tenant-supplied prerequisite and refuses to improvise product-category oversight when it would change the answer.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability Rule and SQF applicability by product or tenant program
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack preserves public traceability and SQFI terminology while requiring runtime confirmation of covered-product scope, certification posture, and tenant retrieval hooks.

notes: The public baseline supports food-manufacturing quality evidence handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for exact product scope, jurisdiction, release owners, rework constraints, supplier approval maps, and customer commitments.
The specialist is intentionally structured as operator-side quality-operations labor and must not imply final QA release, recall command, legal advice, or supplier-approval authority.
